subject: Overflowing garbage cans at rental property on Hirsch Ave
body_clean: Mayor Okonkwo, I'm writing about the rental unit at 2208 Hirsch Ave. The garbage cans there have been overflowing every single week since at least May. Bags are piling up on the parkway and tearing open overnight — I think animals are getting into them. The smell on hot days is awful. I've knocked on the landlord's door twice with no answer. Whoever is responsible for that property needs to be held accountable. Can the Village do something about this before it gets any worse?

subject: Overflowing garbage cans at rental property on Hirsch Ave
body_clean: Ms. Okafor, thank you for reaching out. I've forwarded your concern to both Code Enforcement and our Sanitation division for a site inspection at 2208 Hirsch Ave. Overflowing waste at a rental property can be a code violation, and our officers will contact the property owner directly. You should see activity within the next few business days. Please follow up if the situation hasn't improved by end of next week.

subject: Overflowing garbage cans at rental property on Hirsch Ave
body_clean: Thank you for the quick reply. I walked by yesterday and there is a notice posted on the door at 2208 and the extra bags on the parkway are gone. Much improved. I still don't know if the root problem is fixed but I'll give it a couple weeks and let you know. Really appreciate that someone took this seriously.

subject: Scavengers tearing through recycling bins on Eastern Ave
body_clean: Hi, I live near the 400 block of Eastern Ave and every Tuesday night before recycling pickup somebody goes through everyone's bins pulling out cans and bottles and leaving everything else scattered. By Wednesday morning the whole block has paper and cardboard blowing around. I understand people are trying to make ends meet but the mess is out of control. Is there a Village ordinance against going through recycling? And is there anything you can do to help?

subject: Scavengers tearing through recycling bins on Eastern Ave
body_clean: Mr. Webb, thank you for writing. You're correct that Village ordinance prohibits removing materials from residential recycling bins set out for collection — once curbside, they're technically Village property. I've asked Officer Banks to include the 400 block of Eastern Ave in Tuesday evening patrols for the next several weeks. I appreciate your patient and thoughtful way of framing this. We'll address the littering without making things harder on anyone who's struggling.

subject: Garbage not picked up the week of New Year's
body_clean: Hello Mayor, our garbage was not picked up on Marshall Ave the week of New Year's Day and nobody on our block could figure out the revised holiday schedule. I finally found something on Facebook but it was posted the day before, and I'd already put my cans out. We ended up with two weeks of garbage sitting out in the cold — actually better than summer, but the bins were still overflowing by the time pickup finally came. Could the Village send a postcard or robocall before holiday weeks going forward?

subject: Garbage not picked up the week of New Year's
body_clean: Ms. Truskowski, we really appreciate this feedback and we're sorry for the confusion. You're raising a fair point — a Facebook post the day before isn't enough notice. I'm passing your suggestion along to our communications team and to Public Works, and we'll look at whether a direct mailer or automated phone notification is feasible for major holidays. Thank you for being patient and for taking the time to write.

subject: Need a second recycling cart — current one isn't big enough
body_clean: Mayor Okonkwo, my household at 3115 Washington Blvd has four adults and our single recycling cart is full by Tuesday every single week, sometimes by Sunday. Last week I put overflow bags next to the cart and the truck left them sitting there. Can we request an additional recycling cart from the Village? I'd pay a reasonable fee if needed. We're committed to recycling properly — we just don't have the capacity.

subject: Need a second recycling cart — current one isn't big enough
body_clean: Mr. Pittman, great news — Bellwood does offer a second recycling cart for households that need extra capacity. There is a small annual fee. You can request one by calling Public Works at 708-555-0141 or stopping by Village Hall. Also, flattened cardboard bundled flat beside the cart is picked up by the truck even without a second bin, which may help in the meantime. We'll flag your address so Public Works is aware. Thanks for recycling and for reaching out.

subject: Trash piling up at rental on Bohland Ave — health concern
body_clean: Dear Mayor, I am a longtime Bellwood resident at 1412 Bohland Ave and I need to report a serious situation next door at 1410. That rental property has at least five garbage cans crammed into the side yard, most without lids, and the smell in this heat is making it impossible to be in my backyard. I have seen rats twice this week. I am 71 years old and I shouldn't have to live like this. Please send someone out immediately.

subject: Missed recycling pickup on Rice Ave
body_clean: Hey — the recycling truck skipped my block on Rice Ave (I'm at 2614) last Thursday. My neighbors all had the same issue. We put carts out the night before like we're supposed to and they were still sitting there Friday evening. I ended up pulling mine back in but there were a few carts left out all weekend which looked rough. Can you check what happened and make sure we don't get missed two weeks in a row?

subject: Requesting a second recycling cart for our household
body_clean: Hello, I'm writing about our household at 507 Granville Ave. We have six family members and our single recycling bin is almost always overflowing before pickup day. I've read online that some communities allow a second cart and I'm hoping Bellwood does too. We don't want to throw recyclables in the garbage — we just need more room. Please let me know the process for requesting an additional cart.

subject: Garbage from rental units blowing into my yard on St. Paul Ave
body_clean: Mayor Okonkwo, I have a real problem with the multi-unit rental at 219 St. Paul Ave. They put out way too many bags for the cart size and by Monday morning wind has blown trash all up and down the block. I've picked up other people's chicken bones and diapers from my front lawn three times in the last month. Who is responsible for making the landlord manage their waste properly? I don't want to be a complainer but this has been going on long enough.

subject: Holiday week garbage schedule confusion — MLK Day
body_clean: Hi there, I put my garbage out Monday for MLK Day week and the truck came Tuesday instead, which I didn't know about. My neighbor told me but not until Tuesday afternoon. The cans were out all day on the wrong day and I almost forgot to put them back out Tuesday night. Is there somewhere I can sign up to get a text or email reminder when the pickup schedule shifts? I tried the Village website but couldn't find a notification option.

subject: Scavengers on Monroe St making a mess every week
body_clean: What's up — I live at 1903 Monroe St and every single garbage night someone comes through with a shopping cart hitting every bin on the block. They take what they want but knock lids off and leave bags on the ground. I don't have a problem with people looking for stuff but when they leave garbage scattered in the street that's where it becomes everyone's problem. My neighbor is 80 and can't be picking up trash off the parkway. Is there a community outreach option or does the Village just write tickets?

subject: Garbage cans blocking sidewalk at rental — 50th Ave
body_clean: Dear Mayor Okonkwo, I want to bring to your attention that the rental property at 728 50th Ave puts their garbage cans out on Saturday for Monday pickup and then leaves them on the sidewalk until sometimes Wednesday or Thursday. I have to walk in the street with my stroller to get around them on the way to school, which is dangerous. This has been going on all summer. I've tried talking to the tenants. Is there an ordinance about this?

subject: Second recycling cart request — Frederick Ave
body_clean: Hi Mayor, we moved to Bellwood about a year ago and we love it here. Quick question — our family goes through a lot of cardboard from online orders plus plastic and glass, and the single recycling cart fills up before pickup day almost every time. My friend in Oak Park gets two carts from their village. Does Bellwood have a way to get a second one? I'm at 3308 Frederick Ave. Happy to pay whatever fee is involved. Thanks for everything you do.

subject: Rental property trash situation — Harvard Ave
body_clean: Mayor's office, the rental property at 1108 Harvard Ave has been getting worse all fall. The landlord puts out maybe three small cans for what seems like eight or ten people living there, so by pickup day things are stacked on the ground. Last week with the cold snap bags froze to the pavement and some are still there. I realize this is hard to enforce but it's a genuine health and sanitation issue and I'm hoping Code Enforcement can help.

subject: Bulk item pickup — still waiting three weeks later
body_clean: Mayor, I scheduled a bulk item pickup for early June at 1522 25th Ave and as of this email it has been over three weeks and the old couch and mattress are still sitting in my gangway. I called the number I was given and left two messages with no callback. I had family visiting and it was embarrassing. Can someone please either reschedule me or let me know what's going on with the bulk pickup program?

subject: Six-unit building on St. Charles sharing one recycling bin
body_clean: Hi, I moved into an apartment building at 2019 St. Charles Rd a few months ago and the entire six-unit building shares just one small recycling bin. It is always overflowing by mid-week. I've talked to the property manager who says it's the Village's responsibility to provide more. Can you clarify who is responsible for providing adequate recycling capacity at multi-unit buildings, and is there a way to get additional containers out there?

subject: Garbage cans at rental on Morris Ave — rats and smell
body_clean: Mayor Okonkwo, I am a homeowner at 1716 Morris Ave and the rental directly across at 1713 Morris has had a rodent and garbage problem since spring. They have more garbage than the cans can hold and most cans have no lids. I have personally seen rats during daylight hours, which I understand is a bad sign. I have a six-year-old at home. I am asking Code Enforcement to look at this as soon as possible. I have photos if that would help.

subject: Rental on Avon Ave needs more garbage capacity — blowing trash
body_clean: Dear Mayor, there is a property at 1055 Avon Ave with what looks like a lot of tenant turnover. Every few months a new group moves in and within weeks the garbage situation gets out of hand — bags pile up beyond the cans and this week wind has scattered takeout containers and plastic all the way to my backyard at 1059. I've picked up other people's garbage three times just this week. The landlord needs to either get more cans or be fined. I am at my wit's end.
